intro: Aphelandra rosulata, also known as Gold Dust Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to Costa Rica and Panama. The leaves of Aphelandra rosulata have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
